content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Lost international items posted to the UK

  - url: https://help.royalmail.com/personal/s/article/Lost-international-items-posted-to-the-UK

  - source: Royal Mail

  - domain: help.royalmail.com


  ## Content


  # Lost international items posted to the UK


  If you have issues with incoming international mail, check with the postal service
  or carrier in the country where the item was originally sent.


  This guidance is for international items posted to the UK from abroad. For an issue
  with this kind of incoming mail, the relevant starting point is the postal service
  or carrier in the original sending country.

  '
metadata: {}

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Royal Mail Claims Centre - process and options

  - url: https://help.royalmail.com/personal/s/article/Royal-Mail-Claims-Centre-process-and-options

  - source: Royal Mail

  - domain: help.royalmail.com


  ## Content


  # Royal Mail Claims Centre - process and options


  The Royal Mail Claims Centre provides separate help and claim routes for different
  kinds of issues.


  Available claim topics include:


  - claiming for lost or damaged items;

  - claiming for delayed items;

  - claiming for failure to obtain a signature;

  - claiming for damaged property;

  - providing evidence for a claim.


  Choose the claim topic that matches the issue you are dealing with and follow the
  relevant Claims Centre process.

  '

args.query: site:laposte.fr réclamation envoi international expéditeur La Poste

content.content:
The item was posted in **France by La Poste** and is destined for the **United Kingdom**.

Because it is incoming international mail, **La Poste—the postal service in the country where it was sent—should handle the missing-item enquiry**, rather than Royal Mail. Contact La Poste or ask the sender to open an international investigation/claim using the shipment reference **SHP-UK-10482**.

Royal Mail’s guidance confirms that issues with international items posted abroad should be raised first with the originating country’s postal service.

final_answer:
The item was posted in **France by La Poste** and is destined for the **United Kingdom**.

Because it is incoming international mail, **La Poste—the postal service in the country where it was sent—should handle the missing-item enquiry**, rather than Royal Mail. Contact La Poste or ask the sender to open an international investigation/claim using the shipment reference **SHP-UK-10482**.

Royal Mail’s guidance confirms that issues with international items posted abroad should be raised first with the originating country’s postal service.
